text:
Orders

IT IS ORDERED [BY CONSENT] / [WITH EFFECT FROM [DECREE ABSOLUTE] / [FINAL [DIVORCE] / [DISSOLUTION] ORDER]]:

Lump sum order

The [applicant] / [respondent] shall pay to the [respondent] / [applicant] a lump sum of £[amount] by [time and date] / [[time] on the date [number] days after the date of this order] / [[time] on the date [[number] days after the date] of completion of the sale of [property name] referred to at paragraph [para number] [above] / [below]].

(either, if the lump sum is more than £5,000)

If the [applicant] / [respondent] fails to pay all or any part of this lump sum [by [date for payment]] / [within [number] days of [date for payment]] simple interest shall accrue on the remaining balance of the lump sum at [the rate applicable for the time being to a High Court judgment debt] / [the rate of [percentage] % per annum].

(or, if the lump sum is less than £5,000)

If the [applicant] / [respondent] fails to pay all or any part of this lump sum [by [date for payment]] / [within [number] days of [date for payment]], the [applicant] / [respondent] shall pay to the [respondent] / [applicant] a further lump sum calculated as follows: £[amount] per day from [date for payment] until the lump sum payment referred to at paragraph [para number of a.] above is paid in full.

Series of lump sum orders

The [applicant] / [respondent] shall pay to the [respondent] / [applicant] a series of lump sums:

£[amount] by [time and date] / [[time] on the date [number] days after the date of this order];

£[amount] by [time and date] / [[time] on the date [number] days after the date of this order]; and

£[amount] by [time and date] / [[time] on the date [number] days after the date of this order].

(either, if the lump sum is more than £5,000)

metadata.source: Standard Family Orders — Volume 1: Financial Remedies (May 2026)
